source: Live GitHub REST repository, README, and license endpoints queried with authenticated gh CLI

repositories.feature_basis: Three explicit review modes, isolated per-persona backend calls, evidence gaps, persona/skill routing, stable CLI/API output, and an explicitly structural local backend.
README documents CLI/library evaluation, automated evals, red teaming, vulnerability scanning, and CI/CD checks.
README documents production-grade multi-agent workflows, multiple providers, checkpointing, human-in-the-loop, OpenTelemetry observability, DevUI, and Python/.NET support.
README documents pytest-style LLM evaluation, custom and agentic metrics, task completion, tool correctness, and goal accuracy.

repositories.reported_license: CC-BY-4.0; component-level use needs separate review
repositories.feature_basis: README documents multi-agent orchestration and runtimes but states AutoGen is in maintenance mode and recommends Microsoft Agent Framework for new projects.
Project README and documentation position Ragas as an LLM application, RAG, and agent evaluation metrics framework.

repositories.reported_license: MIT code with separately licensed datasets
repositories.feature_basis: README documents an LLM/system evaluation framework, public eval registry, custom/private evals, and benchmark data; LICENSE lists dataset-specific exceptions.

repositories.reported_license: MIT outside the enterprise directories declared by the repository
repositories.feature_basis: README documents OpenTelemetry tracing, evaluations, datasets, debugging, experiments, and self-hosting; top-level license excludes named enterprise directories.
README documents modular language-model programs and prompt/weight optimization for classifiers, RAG pipelines, and agent loops.

repositories.reported_license: Elastic License 2.0; source-available, not OSI open source
repositories.feature_basis: README documents OpenTelemetry tracing, evaluations, datasets, experiments, prompt playground, and agent debugging; ELv2 restricts hosted-service use.
README documents a unified model-evaluation framework, more than 60 academic benchmarks, task configuration, local models, and reproducible public prompts.
